page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server configured for high-density compute and machine learning workloads. It features eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs providing a total of 637 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 208 vCPUs across 104 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

The instance is equipped with 1872.0 GB of system memory, translating to 9.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server bundles 16 local NVMe SSDs totaling 6167 GB of capacity. This high-density memory and storage configuration is designed to feed data rapidly to the GPU accelerators, minimizing I/O bottlenecks during large-scale training and inference tasks.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top 10%, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth results, such as 13659.34 GB/sec for small-block cached reads and 426.55 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve top-tier throughput, with Redis reaching 179.39 million operations per second. For LLM text generation, the system achieves 28.93 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Given its massive hardware profile, the server offers high resource density, though its specialized nature represents a qualitative tradeoff optimized for high-budget, compute-heavy deployments rather than general-purpose utility.

This server is engineered for demanding workloads such as large language model training and inference, high-performance computing, ray tracing, and large-scale database operations. The combination of eight H100 GPUs, extensive NVMe storage, and high-capacity system memory makes it suitable for deep learning, data science pipelines, and complex physical simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning. It features eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 637 GB of total VRAM, paired with 208 vCPUs on dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors. The system includes 1872.0 GB of RAM and 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks place the server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with strong performance in LLM text generation. This high-density hardware profile is optimized for deep learning, large-scale simulations, and intensive data science workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 208 vCPUs, 1872GB RAM, and 6167GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for top-tier machine learning and HPC workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server with 8 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 208 vCPUs, 1872GB RAM, and 6167GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 637 GB total VRAM
208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1872 GB of system memory
6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for large language model training, inference, and deep learning
